caption:
Universities must remain safe spaces for young people pursuing education.
Many students are away from home, without strong support networks, and navigating pressure alone. If we understand how difficult that journey already is, then this is something we should all be involved in — not just when incidents go viral but consistently.
This campaign exists to help stamp down harm early, create safe spaces, and ensure young people have clear routes to report concerns so communities can intervene before situations escalate.
We haven’t posted extensively about this incident — and that’s intentional. When young people are involved, safeguarding matters more than views, likes, or online traction.
Since the incident, Forever Family and SOS Protection Limited have helped to facilitate two community meetings in Leicester, with a primary focus on directly supporting the young people most affected and working with families, professionals, and local partners to build a tangible, long-term support structure.
The model being developed in Leicester is designed to be scalable — not only for Leicester University but for other universities and communities facing similar realities.
Thank you to:
• @zampaladus for making a platinum referral and bringing the family down
• @shareefaenergy for hosting the meeting and guiding us across the universities
• @sosprotectionltd for mobilising members and providing continued on-the-ground support
• @acexchange for inspiring and powering the step to protect young people in education
• All professionals and community members involved in delivering tangible support and long-term solutions
This is how safeguarding is built — together.

#forever

@foreverfamilyuk would like to thank everyone who shared our message and mission, everyone who spoke up for the voiceless, and everyone who stood up for those who may find it difficult to stand up for themselves. 

Social media can easily have us sharing and engaging in the problems in the community. At foreverfamilyuk, we pledge to never speak about problems without offering tangible solutions. 

If you want to do more than promote the problem and you also wish to empower others with tangible solutions, feel free to share this post. You are welcome to add any other solutions in the comments as well. 

Thank you for your continued support. Together, we can build the institutions that empower and protect communities. #forever

Share this everywhere and make sure everyone sees the WE made history! Share this and debunk the narrative of fear and division. Share this and show the world how good people coming together has the power to shut down threats of violence and preserve the peace ♾️

@foreverfamilyuk communicated  with local people, community organisations and institutions and had FF and SOS operatives in every area to ensure protection and safety across the community 

1 @foreverfamilyuk @gangsunitecic @streetfathers @faronalexpaul @fazamnesty_uncut Provided collective security for peace, unity, safety and protection of the community in the areas that were under threat 

2. @raspectrebellionff speaking in Walthamstow 

3. @faronalexpaul speaking in Walthamstow 

4. @viczofficial1 speaking in Lewisham 

5. @naba_sage_el_moor speaking in Lewisham 

6. @manlikenabz speaking in Croydon

7. @project_lifeline_ maintaining the peace in Croydon 

8. @streetfathers @gangsunitecic standing in solidarity #deportfargesteeth

9. #touchonetouchall 

10 #everyonevsrascism 

Special thank you to @tripplessecurityldn and all the other close body protection and security operatives who were in Croydon, Lewisham and Walthamstow to protect the community 

Special thank you to @lewishammosque for facilitating unity in the community and demonstrating interfaith solidarity in the face of violence and adversity 

Special thank you to everyone who has stood up, spoke up and stepped out, no matter where you are 

Feel free to share this post and click the link in our bio to support @foreverfamilyuk and more content like this. Thank you for your continued encouragement and assistance.

#foreverfamilyuk

Today is Forever Family's one year anniversary. 12months ago at "The Battle Of Trafalgar" Forever Family was born to serve and protect our communities in the battle against racism, inequality and injustice. Over the past year Forever Family has made great progress, from providing food and clothing to people in need, handing out school supplies to children going back to school, nationwide patrols in response to reports of missing children and kidnappings, to helping guide our youth out of trouble and on to a better path, plus much more. We thank everyone who has supported and helped us to get where we are today. FOREVER...!

#ForeverFamilyUk #ForeverFamilyFund #ItTakesAVillage #WeAreTheVillage #foreverfamilyuk

Patrol your neighbourhood with friends, walk about with your family, go for a late night cruise, link up with FF Force, but most importantly stay vigilant and look out for one another, especially those who are vulnerable 

It takes a village and we are the village.

We aim to continually improve our community service so please let us know what you think, your feedback and support is truly valued. 
Please share our newsletter to your family and friends, and give us your feedback on our platforms (if we make any changes you suggest we’ll let you know 😁).

Do you have warm clothing that you can donate to someone that needs it more? 

Forever Family are collecting items for redistribution to those who are in need of essentials at this time.

This Saturday and Sunday, we will be present in North, East, South and West London to recieve donations of essential wear that will be redistributed to the homeless, vulnerable and elderly in the community. 

If you would like to donate warm clothing and essential wear, please contact 
community@foreverfamilyfund.co.uk

If you would like to donate Food and Essential Items, we will be collecting these at a later date. Please contact Admin@foreverfamilyfund.co.uk 

If you wish to support this initiative financially and wish receive updates about Forever Family, please donate via the paypal link in our bio.

Forever Forever would like to thank all members of the community who have donated towards this initiative. Together we are making a difference in the world.

If you would like to donate warm clothing and essential wear please contact  Community@foreverfamilyfund.co.uk

Clapham Junction Station: 8am, Wednesday 18th November 2020

 Forever Family began its patrol at Clapham Junction train station where the team met to oversee children and young people travelling in the area to school and college. Clapham Junction is a busy area for commuting with numerous bus routes and train lines connecting many areas in and out of London.

Forever Family Force spent time in the area engaging with commuters and local residents. One person who stopped and spoke with Forever Family was Marsha De Cordova, the Labour MP for Battersea and Shadow Secretary of State for women and equalities. She praised the organisation on the community engagement being carried out and made recommendations of local schools and colleges to contact and visit. 

Once the morning rush had ended, Forever Family Force made their way towards St John’s Hill speaking to shopkeepers and residents along the route. Local  Councillor Piers McCausland of the Conservative party spent time talking with some team members. He was interested to hear about our prospects. 

During the afternoon patrol Forever Family Force passed by several schools and colleges along the way, however, due to bad weather interaction with young people at lunchtime break was not as successful as previous patrols.  Forever Family Force utilised this time by engaging with members of the public and spoke to community members, two of whom spoke passionately about the racial disparities and stereotypes directed towards black children in the media. When returning to Clapham Junction there was notably a vast amount of members of the public who were homeless and sleeping in the area. Forever Family Force had many conversations with this community and purchased meals for those who were hungry. One man disclosed his ongoing dilemma of sometimes having to chose between eating or paying for a hostel for the night. 

Another homeless man who sat outside the station, with no shoes on his feet during this wet and cold day, shared in depth how he had ended up homeless and the team assisted him and gifted him with warm clothing.
Throughout the day locals shared their gratitude and support for Forever Family’s continued work
Forever Family Statement: December 2020

Stockwell Station, 8am. Monday 16th November 2020

Forever Family received several messages from concerned parents in the Stockwell area, where a resident had been observed on numerous occasions, photographing and video recording children on their way to and from school. 

A claim was also made that the resident had tried to solicit two schoolgirls to "come upstairs" and that he "blows kisses" to children from his balcony, which faces a Primary School. 

During the patrol many parents spoke to FF Force about their frustrations regarding this Safeguarding concern. The parents had reported the incident to the police, they visited the accused who denied the claims. The police sent out letters to parents and residents in an attempt to reassure them that the situation was under control. However despite this, the accused continued to stand out on his balcony in the morning and afternoon continuing this unwanted behaviour and antagonising distressed parents.

Forever Family patrolled the estate of the accused before and after school and spoke directly to the &accused w he initiated conversation with the team from his balcony. The accused acontinued to deny the allegations made against him. 

Forever Family can report that since our patrol the accused has not been out on his balcony photographing and video recording children in the community.

At lunchtime the team patrolled the area between Stockwell and Brixton speaking to students from nearby Colleges and Schools regarding Forever Family initiatives as-well-as discussing and handing out information about Stop and Search rights. 
Outside Lambeth college in Brixton Forever Family engaged with the students and discussed how important education is and the importance of staying in school. 

Upon returning to Brixton the team spent time talking to residents and workers in the area. One lady revealed that her son had sadly witnessed the murder of his friend. She enquired about Forever Family’s plans to engage with the youth and future incentives stating that her son would love to be part of the Forever Family Force team.

The residents in the community were grateful for Forever Family’s continued presence in the area.

Shepherd's Bush Station: 8am, Friday13th November 2020
 
Forever Family began their outreach from Shepherd Bush Station, West London. Whilst at the tube station, security personnel informed Forever Family Force about local County Lines issues that were present in the community and the effect it has on young teenagers, particularly boys.

On patrol, Forever Family spoke with the local Metropolitan Police regarding Forever Family initiatives, their duty of upholding Stop and Search rights as public servants, and enforcing Covid-19 regulations. 

Forever Family made their way to Hammersmith and West London college via Hammersmith High Street where discussions with local businesses highlighted the need for Forever Family Force patrols. 
People working in the area mentioned the need for Forever Family  to show a visible presence in West London.

Whilst handing out leaflets and engaging with the locals Forever Family spoke with local people experiencing homelessness regarding the issues they face and assisted them by purchasing their lunches 

Forever Family spoke to students and teachers outside of the college regarding the organisation's aims and objectives and demonstrated a knife disarming self-defence technique.

Forever Family are becoming a solid presence across London Boroughs.  Many of the youths which were spoken to already knew of Forever Family and commended them for helping to support the community. 

Forever Family Force ended the day by visiting  and supporting the Ankh Wellbeing Centre @ankhwellbeingcentre in Willesden North West London. 

#TakesAVillage #WeAreTheVillage #foreverfamilyuk

Tottenham: 7am Wednesday 11th November 2020

Forever Family Force met at Seven Sisters tube station in North London to patrol the Tottenham area. It was there that Raspect met with Faron @faronalexpaul , the creator of Faz Amnesty , a confidential initiative set up to offer young people an incentive to surrender knives and other weapons. The movement has seen hundreds of dangerous weapons removed from our communities and out of the hands of young people.

The team, along with Faron patrolled the area surrounding the tube station and engaged with the public on the school run. The team circled the Tottenham Hale Retail Park where they spoke with some young people in the area getting lunch. Some recognised the Family and spoke about concerns in their local area, including knife and gang crime. The team expressed the benefit of having basic self-defence and first aid skills.

Forever Family Force was invited to two locally owned food establishments Electric Grubb @grubb_diner and Jerk Munchies @jerkmunchies whose owners provided the team with delicious food and drinks to keep them fuelled for the community patrol. The support and encouragement from the local community and businesses is gratefully welcomed.

The team then met Mac Impact, founder of BikeStormz and rider Trizzy. BikeStormz is the biggest youth ride out in the UK and promotes a message of peace and positivity for young people.

The team patrolled the route towards Tottenham College where they spoke to young people enrolled on music, business and engineering courses and emphasised the importance of gaining qualifications that can be used in later life.

Towards the end of the evening the team were called to Stratford, East London to support a family whose young daughter was harassed by an older man on her way to school that morning. Forever Family Force made their presence felt in the area to show unity with the family and to show the public Forever Family is here to deter illicit activities and behaviour in all communities.

#ItTakesAVillage #WeAreTheVillage #foreverfamilyuk

Thornton Heath: 7am, Monday 9th November 2020

In response to the stabbings of two local school girls and safety concerns for young people, Forever Family patrolled in Thornton Heath before school to provide security support and assistance for the local community.
Forever Family engaged in discussion with the public and shared their vision and ideas of future plans to set up community-led school safety patrols in areas across London.

Whilst in Thornton Heath, Forever Family Force met a local man who needed financial support for food and drink, which they were able to assist him with as well as carrying his shopping across the road and assisted him with getting onto public transport  safely. The Forever Family Force Outreach Team aim to help the community wherever they can on patrol.

The team headed to West Croydon visiting local Caribbean restaurant, Brixton before walking through Croydon Town Centre where they spoke to passing students about staying safe. Forever Family members demonstrated some self-defence techniques and highlighted the importance of being vigilant. 

Outside the college Forever Family Force were joined by Mahamed Hashi, co founder of @brixtonsoupkitchen who provided information leaflets for young people about their Stop and Search rights. Students were extremely positive in their feedback on Forever Family's presence and their patrols over the past few weeks. 

Whilst in West Croydon the team diffused a situation escalating between two females. The disagreement arose from a negative interaction and tempers flaring in what was perceived to be a lack of respect for an elder. Through conversation and communication, Forever Family Force highlighted the need to have respect for one another in our communities and to lead by example. 

Throughout the day Forever Family were visited by many members of the community showing their support. Some driving by and hooting their horns in encouragement, whilst others blessed the team with refreshments. The patrol ended with an invitation to @bluejaycafe where the team was warmly received. 

Forever Family is overwhelmed by the generosity shown by the community #foreverfamilyuk

GOOD NEWS: We are finally opening the doors for recruitment welcoming people to join the inner Forever Family circle.
If you would like to apply for the Security, Finance, Legal or Media team please email us Admin@ForeverFamilyFund.co.uk you can Dm us too with a note of which sector you would like to be involved in.

On Sat 1st Aug 2020 the brothers and sisters from @ForeverFamilyuk marched from Clapham Common to  Windrush Square in Brixton where they met with other groups unified in the stance to protect our senior and junior generations. Additionally their goal was to emphasize their proud support of Afrikan Emancipation Days, joining the Reparations March and to remember the contribution the Black diaspora has played in shaping this country from the 1800s through to present day!

The work taking place behind the scenes with Forever Family is unprecedented in areas of support for the wider black community. From social and mental health care, legal, financial, educational and political support and a plethora of other support networks designed to bolster communities left to fend for themselves during the most challenging times.

This video is a personal piece shot and edited by @DJRon_Film as a tribute to the efforts of FOREVER FAMILY and in the hope that as we come to the end of Black History Month in the UK, we keep in mind that the work for fairness and equality continues far beyond October 31st both in this country and around the World.

www.ForeverFamilyFund.co.uk
#ForeverFamilyUK 

Message from Ron to Toben: 
God bless @tobenwigwe for the music used in this film from his ‘The Pandemic Experience’ project. Believe me when i say that when i first heard the whole song, everytime I got to the end I cried…4 times in a row! Check out everything Tobie Nwigwe at www.tobenwigwe.com

Forever Family back to school outreach 

“They say it takes a village, so let’s create the village”

@foreverfamilyuk 

#foreverfamilyforce #foreverfamilyfund #foreverfamilyuk

“Forever Family back to school outreach - helping families in need - We Are the Village”

 The uniqueness of Forever Family lies in the authenticity of its members, who are made up of ordinary people who have life experience of the very issues FF fight for. One member, Sian @__its_me_sp__ mother of three, initiated the back to school outreach. She very quickly galvanized a team within Forever Family via our robust networking system. The call was put out to raise funds from within FF, for school equipment, pens, books, pencil cases etc and within less than two weeks the FF back to school outreach team were ready to execute. The team took to the streets of Brixton and used the engagement to conduct surveys on other key areas of need within that community. Plans are now underway to extend the initiative across the school year and meet those needs accordingly. Giving low income families much needed support, particularly during these increasing times of austerity. Our outreach initiative is a perfect example of co-operative economics. It demonstrates what can be achieved by our community if we focus our resources and work together. 

“They say it takes a village, so let’s create the village”

Forever Family Support Black Pound Day 
@ForeverFamilyUK @BPDOfficial 
The spending power of the UK Black community has been valued at £300 Billion. 
Its time to reinvest the wealth and circulate this within the community, by supporting Black owned business

Black Pound Day (BPD) is a solution based approach to supporting the long-term economic growth of UK Black Owned Business. Black Pound Day is a movement, not a moment. Forever Family support this initiative. This is an opportunity to connect with Black Business Owners and support them over the long-term. 
Starting with one day per month, Black Pound Day encourages everyone to spend money with local and online UK Black owned businesses.

5 Simple things to do on Black Pound Day

1. Buy from Black owned business 
2. Take a picture of your purchase, receipt or business
3. Post and Hashtag #BlackPoundDay
4. Share your experience on your socials
5. Recommend the business to a friend

Visit www.blackpoundday.uk to find businesses locally or online. 
Forever Family is a Black owned business. If you wish to pledge your support for the initiative please contact us at www.foreverfamilyfund.co.uk
#ForeverFamilyFund #ForeverFamilyUK #BlackPoundDay
